w2._note: Gross W2 salary ages 47-64 (working years). ~$318.5K taxable → 24% marginal bracket MFJ.
Partial year W2 age 65 (transition to retirement).

ordinary_other._note: Social Security taxable portion (85% of ~$60K combined). Spouse SS starts age 70, self SS starts age 70 — using combined from age 66 (conservative).

readme.purpose: Income from sources OUTSIDE the simulator accounts. Portfolio accounts handle their own distributions internally — do NOT re-enter those here.

format.ages: Recommended. Use age ranges: '47-64' means ages 47 through 64 inclusive. Converted to simulation years using current_age from person.json.
format.years: Legacy format. Year 1 = current_age + 1. Still supported for backward compatibility.

readme.what_not_to_enter: Do NOT enter: dividends or gains from accounts in allocation_yearly.json, RMDs (computed automatically), Roth conversion amounts. Entering these causes double-counting.
readme.gross_amounts: All amounts are GROSS (pre-tax). The simulator computes federal, state, NIIT taxes.
readme.tax_stacking_note: Income here stacks at the BOTTOM of the tax bracket — below RMDs and Roth conversions. W2 $350K fills to 24% bracket during working years. After retirement, SS-only income ($51K taxable = $19.5K after std deduction) leaves huge headroom for Roth conversions.

profile_notes.ages_47_64: W2 $350K → taxable $318.5K → 24% marginal. Roth conversion headroom limited but still valuable vs future 35%+ RMD bracket.
profile_notes.ages_66_74: SS $51K taxable → $19.5K after std deduction → 10% bracket. Prime Roth conversion window — $375K/yr headroom to 24% bracket.
profile_notes.ages_75_95: RMDs dominate. SS + RMDs likely push to 35%+ bracket.

income_types.w2: Gross wages or salary. Ordinary income rates.
income_types.rental: Net rental income after expenses. Ordinary income rates.
income_types.qualified_div: Qualified dividends from outside accounts. LTCG rates.
income_types.cap_gains: Realized capital gains from outside assets. LTCG rates.
